content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "document-management",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 38,
    "domain_depth": 35,
    "data_richness": 30,
    "interaction_quality": 32,
    "ai_integration": 25,
    "mobile_first": 22,
    "performance": 40,
    "typescript_quality": 55,
    "error_handling": 42,
    "realtime": 35,
    "accessibility": 18,
    "security": 45,
    "differentiation": 20,
    "enterprise_readiness": 30
  },
  "overall_score": 33,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "document-management-C1-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "Drawings page missing ARIA labels on interactive elements and discipline filter pills",
      "file": "src/pages/Drawings.tsx",
      "description": "The Drawings page renders discipline filter pills, drawing cards, and action buttons without aria-label or role attributes. Screen readers cannot identify what each pill does, what each drawing card represents, or what actions are available. The upload button and search input also lack accessible labels.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Drawings.tsx, make these specific changes: In the main component function, find all discipline filter pill/button elements and add role=\"button\" and aria-label={`Filter by ${discipline} discipline`} and aria-pressed={isActive}. Find the search input and add aria-label=\"Search drawings by sheet number or title\". Find each drawing card container and add role=\"article\" and aria-label={`Drawing ${drawing.setNumber} ${drawing.disciplineLabel}`}. Find the upload/add button and add aria-label=\"Upload new drawing\". BEFORE: Interactive elements render without ARIA attributes, failing axe-core audit. AFTER: All interactive elements have proper ARIA labels, roles, and states for screen reader accessibility. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "document-management-C1-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "interaction_quality",
      "title": "Files page has no loading skeleton, shows blank content flash on initial fetch",
      "file": "src/pages/Files.tsx",
      "description": "The Files page does not render a skeleton loader while data is being fetched. Users see a blank white area for 500ms to 3s before content appears. The PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA Global Standards require skeleton screens matching final layout geometry on every page, never a blank screen.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Files.tsx, make these specific changes: Import the existing Skeletons component from src/components/ui/Skeletons.tsx. In the main component function, find where the loading state is checked (isLoading or loading variable). BEFORE: When loading is true, either nothing is rendered or a bare spinner is shown, causing a blank content flash. AFTER: When loading is true, render a skeleton layout that matches the final file list geometry: a row of 4 skeleton metric cards (height 80px, border-radius 12px) at the top, then 8 skeleton table rows (height 48px each) below, all using the pulse animation from the existing Skeletons component. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "document-management-C1-003",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "DrawingViewer component not usable on mobile — no pinch-to-zoom, toolbar overflows viewport",
      "file": "src/components/drawings/DrawingViewer.tsx",
      "description": "The DrawingViewer renders a fixed-width toolbar and canvas that overflows on screens under 768px. Touch gestures for pan and zoom are not implemented. The markup toolbar buttons are too small for touch (under 44px). Field superintendents cannot view or annotate drawings on their phones, which is a core field-first requirement.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/drawings/DrawingViewer.tsx, make these specific changes: 1) Find the outer container div and change its style to include width: '100%', maxWidth: '100vw', overflow: 'hidden'. 2) Find the toolbar container and add a media query style: on screens below 768px, set flexWrap: 'wrap', padding: '8px', and ensure each button has minWidth: '44px' and minHeight: '44px' for touch targets. 3) Find the canvas/viewer container and add touchAction: 'none' to enable custom touch handling, and set width: '100%', height: 'calc(100vh - 120px)' so it fills the mobile viewport. 4) Add an onTouchStart/onTouchMove handler stub (even if placeholder) that prevents the toolbar from blocking the viewport. BEFORE: DrawingViewer overflows horizontally on mobile, toolbar buttons are ~24px (too small for touch), no touch gesture support. AFTER: DrawingViewer fills mobile viewport without overflow, toolbar wraps and buttons meet 44px minimum touch target, container is ready for touch gestures. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "document-management-C1-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "Document upload failures silently swallowed — no toast or retry affordance",
      "file": "src/components/files/UploadZone.tsx",
      "description": "When a file upload fails (network error, size limit, unsupported type), the UploadZone component catches the error but does not display a user-visible notification or retry button. The user sees the upload progress disappear with no explanation. PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA requires toast notification plus inline retry button on network failures.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/files/UploadZone.tsx, make these specific changes: Find the catch block in the upload handler function (likely onDrop or handleUpload). BEFORE: The catch block logs to console.error but does not show any UI feedback to the user. AFTER: In the catch block, 1) check if a toast/notification function is available (import from existing notification system or use window.alert as fallback), 2) display an error message: 'Upload failed: [error.message]. Please try again.' 3) Set an error state variable (e.g., setUploadError(error.message)) that renders an inline error message with a 'Retry' button below the drop zone. The retry button should call the upload function again with the same file(s). Also add aria-live=\"polite\" to the error message container so screen readers announce the failure. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "document-management-C1-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "domain_depth",
      "title": "Drawings page missing revision history timeline and version comparison per acceptance criteria",
      "file": "src/pages/Drawings.tsx",
      "description": "PAGE_ACCEPTANCE_CRITERIA for Drawings requires: revision history timeline of all revisions per sheet with change summary, and side-by-side comparison for revisions. The getDrawingRevisionHistory endpoint exists in src/api/endpoints/documents.ts and VersionCompare component exists in src/components/drawings/VersionCompare.tsx, but neither is wired into the Drawings page. Users cannot see revision history or compare versions.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/pages/Drawings.tsx, make these specific changes: 1) Import getDrawingRevisionHistory from '../../api/endpoints/documents' (adjust path as needed) and the VersionCompare component from '../../components/drawings/VersionCompare'. 2) In the drawing detail view or when a drawing is selected, add a 'Revision History' section that calls getDrawingRevisionHistory(drawingId) and renders each revision as a timeline item showing revision_number, issued_date, change_description, and issued_by. 3) Add a 'Compare Versions' button that, when clicked, opens the VersionCompare component with the current and previous revision file URLs. BEFORE: Revision history and version comparison are not rendered on the Drawings page despite backend support and component existence. AFTER: Selected drawing shows a revision timeline and a Compare Versions button that opens the VersionCompare overlay. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [],
  "competitive_intel": "Procore offers a plan room with revision tracking and basic markup but lacks AI conflict detection. PlanGrid (now ACC) has superior mobile drawing markup with Apple Pencil support. Fieldwire pins tasks to drawings with offline support. SiteSync has the components (DrawingViewer, MarkupToolbar, VersionCompare, PdfViewer) but they are not wired together into a cohesive workflow. The revision history API exists but is not surfaced in the UI. Mobile drawing viewing is broken due to overflow and missing touch gestures, which is a dealbreaker for field adoption.",
  "summary": "The Document Management module scores 33/100. The backend infrastructure is reasonably solid — typed API endpoints for drawings and files exist with proper error transformation and project scoping. However, the frontend is severely underbuilt for a field-first construction platform. Critical gaps include: no skeleton loaders on the Files page (blank flash violates global standards), the DrawingViewer is completely unusable on mobile (overflows, no touch targets, no pinch-to-zoom), upload failures are silently swallowed with no user feedback, ARIA labels are missing across all interactive elements, and the revision history/version comparison features exist as components but are never rendered on the actual Drawings page. The five surgical fixes target the most impactful gaps: accessibility compliance, loading states, mobile responsiveness, error handling, and wiring existing revision tracking into the UI."
}
```
